31.problem_description: Extract the last n characters from a string and repeat them n times, returning the concatenated result.

kcs.name: String substring extraction with index
kcs.reasoning: All solutions use substring(str.length() - n) to extract the final n characters from the input string.

kcs.name: Loop-based string repetition pattern
kcs.reasoning: Every solution uses a for-loop that iterates n times to repeat the extracted substring.

kcs.name: String concatenation in iteration
kcs.reasoning: Solutions build the result by repeatedly concatenating the substring using += or + operators within the loop.

kcs.name: Index calculation for string length
kcs.reasoning: All solutions compute str.length() - n to correctly identify the starting position for the last n characters.

kcs.name: Method parameter and return typing
kcs.reasoning: The method accepts String and int parameters, requiring proper understanding of non-primitive and primitive type signatures.

32.problem_description: Replace all characters not part of occurrences of a target word with '+' symbols, preserving the word where it appears in the original string.

kcs.name: Character-by-character string traversal with index
kcs.reasoning: All solutions iterate through the string using a while loop with an index variable that advances based on match conditions.

kcs.name: Substring extraction and comparison at position
kcs.reasoning: Solutions check if a substring starting at the current index matches the target word using substring() and equals().

kcs.name: Boundary condition for substring access
kcs.reasoning: All solutions verify i <= str.length() - word.length() before extracting a substring to prevent index out-of-bounds errors.

kcs.name: Conditional index advancement logic
kcs.reasoning: The index advances by word length when a match occurs, or by 1 otherwise, controlling the traversal behavior.

kcs.name: StringBuilder/StringBuffer for efficient string building
kcs.reasoning: Solutions 1-4 use mutable string buffers to append characters incrementally rather than creating new string objects repeatedly.

kcs.name: String type conversion from buffer
kcs.reasoning: Solutions using StringBuffer or StringBuilder must call toString() to convert the accumulated buffer back to a String type for return.

33.problem_description: Interleave characters from two strings alternately, then append any remaining characters from the longer string.

kcs.name: String character access with charAt
kcs.reasoning: All solutions use charAt(index) to retrieve individual characters from strings for interleaving.

kcs.name: Index-based loop traversal of strings
kcs.reasoning: All solutions use for loops with index variable to iterate through string positions simultaneously.

kcs.name: String length comparison for control flow
kcs.reasoning: Solutions must compare string lengths (via length(), Math.min, or conditional branching) to handle unequal inputs correctly.

kcs.name: String concatenation or StringBuilder append
kcs.reasoning: Solutions either concatenate strings directly or use StringBuilder to build the result incrementally.

kcs.name: Substring extraction for tail handling
kcs.reasoning: All solutions extract and append the remaining characters from the longer string using substring() after the main loop.

kcs.name: Conditional branching for edge cases
kcs.reasoning: Several solutions explicitly handle empty strings or equal/unequal length cases with if/else structures.

34.problem_description: Find all occurrences of 'z' followed by any character then 'p' in a string, and replace each three-character pattern with 'zp', effectively removing the middle character.

kcs.name: String character access via charAt()
kcs.reasoning: Most solutions use charAt() to directly examine individual characters at specific indices for efficient pattern matching.

kcs.name: Loop-based string traversal with index
kcs.reasoning: All solutions iterate through the string using for or while loops with manual index management to examine each position.

kcs.name: Multi-condition pattern detection with AND
kcs.reasoning: Solutions check two independent conditions (z at position i, p at position i+2) combined with logical AND to identify the target pattern.

kcs.name: Bounds checking before indexed access
kcs.reasoning: All solutions verify i < str.length() - 2 before accessing str.charAt(i+2) to prevent index out-of-bounds exceptions.

kcs.name: Conditional index increment in loops
kcs.reasoning: When a pattern matches, index increments by 3 to skip the matched segment; otherwise it increments by 1, requiring selective loop control.

kcs.name: String building with StringBuilder or concatenation
kcs.reasoning: Solutions construct the result by appending characters or substrings, using either StringBuilder (efficient) or string concatenation (simpler).

36.problem_description: Count the number of occurrences of the pattern 'c', 'o', any character, 'e' (c_o_e) in a string.

kcs.name: String traversal with index-based loop
kcs.reasoning: All solutions iterate through the string using a for loop with index i to examine substrings starting at each position.

kcs.name: Loop boundary calculation with pattern length
kcs.reasoning: All solutions use str.length() - 3 as the upper bound to ensure i+3 remains within valid string indices for a 4-character pattern.

kcs.name: Character-by-character pattern matching
kcs.reasoning: Solutions verify specific characters at fixed offsets (i, i+1, i+3) using charAt() or substring methods to identify the pattern.

kcs.name: Wildcard position in pattern matching
kcs.reasoning: The pattern skips checking position i+2, allowing any character to match at that location within the overall 4-character sequence.

kcs.name: Accumulator counter variable
kcs.reasoning: All solutions initialize an integer counter to zero and increment it each time the complete pattern is found, returning the total count.

kcs.name: Conjunctive boolean conditions
kcs.reasoning: Multiple character/substring checks are combined with && operators to ensure all parts of the pattern must match simultaneously.

37.problem_description: Determine whether one string ends with another string (case-insensitive), returning true if either string is a suffix of the other.

kcs.name: Case-insensitive string comparison
kcs.reasoning: All solutions normalize case using toLowerCase(), equalsIgnoreCase(), or compareTo() to handle mixed-case input correctly.

kcs.name: String length retrieval and calculation
kcs.reasoning: Solutions 2-5 retrieve string lengths and compute substring boundaries to extract suffixes for comparison.

kcs.name: Substring extraction for suffix matching
kcs.reasoning: Solutions 2-5 use substring() to extract the last N characters from the longer string to match against the shorter.

kcs.name: String equality comparison methods
kcs.reasoning: Solutions use equals(), compareTo(), equalsIgnoreCase(), or endsWith() to test string equivalence with different approaches.

kcs.name: Conditional branching on length comparison
kcs.reasoning: Solutions 2-5 branch logic based on which string is longer to determine the extraction source and comparison target.

kcs.name: Boolean return from method logic
kcs.reasoning: All solutions return true or false based on the suffix-matching condition, requiring correct boolean type handling.

38.problem_description: Determine whether a string contains 'xyz' as a substring, but only if 'xyz' is NOT immediately preceded by a period character.

kcs.name: String search with indexOf method
kcs.reasoning: Solutions use indexOf() to locate target substring 'xyz' and '.xyz' patterns within the string, often with position offsets.

kcs.name: Substring extraction and comparison
kcs.reasoning: Solutions extract 3-character windows using substring(i, i+3) and compare with equals() to verify exact matches.

kcs.name: Loop bounds with string length
kcs.reasoning: Iterative solutions carefully set loop limits (length - 2 or length - 3) to safely access 3-character substrings without index bounds errors.

kcs.name: Character inspection with charAt
kcs.reasoning: Solutions check the character immediately before 'xyz' using charAt() to verify it is not a period.

kcs.name: Negative constraint enforcement
kcs.reasoning: Solutions combine presence checks ('xyz' found) with negation checks (not preceded by '.') using conditional logic and boolean operators.

kcs.name: Recursion with string subdivision
kcs.reasoning: Some solutions use recursive calls on substring parts to handle the forbidden pattern by splitting and rechecking subproblems.

39.problem_description: Determine if a string is x-y balanced, where balance means no 'x' appears after the last 'y' (or neither character exists).

kcs.name: String character access with charAt
kcs.reasoning: Most solutions use charAt(index) to examine individual characters at specific positions in the string.

kcs.name: Backward iteration through string indices
kcs.reasoning: Solutions 1, 2, 5 traverse the string from end to beginning using decremented loop counter to find the rightmost character match.

kcs.name: Early return from method with boolean
kcs.reasoning: All solutions return immediately upon finding a decisive character ('x' or 'y'), avoiding unnecessary remaining iterations.

kcs.name: String position search methods
kcs.reasoning: Solution 3 uses indexOf() and lastIndexOf() to locate character positions and compare them for balance logic.

kcs.name: Substring and contains string methods
kcs.reasoning: Solution 4 uses substring() to extract remaining string portion and contains() to search for 'y' after 'x'.

kcs.name: Default case return statement
kcs.reasoning: All solutions return true as the implicit default when the loop completes without encountering a problematic 'x' character.

40.problem_description: Extract the substring between the first and last occurrences of 'bread' in a string, returning an empty string if 'bread' appears fewer than two times.

kcs.name: String indexOf and lastIndexOf methods
kcs.reasoning: Solutions 1, 2, 4 use indexOf() to find first occurrence and lastIndexOf() to find last occurrence of the target substring.

kcs.name: Sentinel value pattern for search results
kcs.reasoning: All solutions recognize that indexOf returns -1 when not found and validate against this to handle missing or single occurrences.

kcs.name: Substring extraction with index calculation
kcs.reasoning: All solutions use substring(start, end) with adjusted indices (first+5 to skip 'bread') to extract the middle content.

kcs.name: Edge case handling for duplicate positions
kcs.reasoning: All solutions check if first and last occurrence indices are equal to detect insufficient 'bread' instances and return empty string.

kcs.name: Loop-based string pattern matching
kcs.reasoning: Solutions 3, 5 use loops with substring comparison to manually locate 'bread' instead of built-in search methods.

kcs.name: Early exit from loop patterns
kcs.reasoning: Solutions 3, 5 use break or loop variable manipulation to exit loops once the target substring is found.

128.problem_description: Detect whether a string contains the pattern 'bob' (letter 'b', any character, letter 'b') and return true if found, false otherwise.

kcs.name: String character access with charAt()
kcs.reasoning: Solutions 1-4 require indexing into strings to compare individual characters at specific positions.

kcs.name: Loop boundary adjustment for patterns
kcs.reasoning: All solutions must iterate only up to str.length() - 2 to ensure a 3-character window exists without index overflow.

kcs.name: Multi-condition AND logic in conditionals
kcs.reasoning: The pattern detection requires both charAt(i) == 'b' AND charAt(i+2) == 'b', combining two independent character checks.

kcs.name: Early exit with return statement
kcs.reasoning: Solutions immediately return true upon finding the first 'bob' occurrence, avoiding unnecessary continued iteration.

kcs.name: Fixed offset indexing in loops
kcs.reasoning: Solutions use index arithmetic (i+2) to check characters at fixed distances, treating strings as fixed-width pattern matching.

kcs.name: Default false return after loop
kcs.reasoning: All solutions return false if the loop completes without finding a match, handling the negative case.
